entries.title: October kickoff brain dump
entries.body: Everything rattling around in my head this morning: the project deadline, the call I keep postponing, the book half-read on my nightstand. Letting it all out here so I can actually focus. My brain feels like seventeen browser tabs open at once. Writing helps collapse them into something manageable.

entries.title: Gratitude — small things
entries.body: Grateful for the smell of coffee this morning, for the neighbour who held the elevator, for ten uninterrupted minutes to read. These feel trivial but they stack up. A day built from small kindnesses is quietly a good day. I want to notice these more instead of chasing bigger wins.

entries.title: Morning run — week 2
entries.body: Ran 4km without stopping for the first time. Legs were heavy after the first kilometre but I pushed through. Heart rate settled around 155 by the halfway point. Felt strong at the end, which is new. Going to stick with every other day for another two weeks before upping the distance.

entries.title: Vivid dream — the old house
entries.body: Dreamed I was back in my childhood home but the rooms kept rearranging. The kitchen led into a library that didn't exist. My grandmother was there, younger than I ever knew her. Woke up with a strange ache. Dreams like this leave residue. Spent breakfast just sitting with it.

entries.title: Finished Atomic Habits
entries.body: Finished Atomic Habits tonight. The two-minute rule is deceptively simple — it makes starting frictionless. The identity piece hit hardest: you don't rise to the level of your goals, you fall to the level of your systems. Want to reread chapter 4 and actually map my existing habits before adding new ones.

entries.title: Weekly bullets — Oct 19
entries.body: Done: reviewed Q4 plan, called mum, fixed the leaky tap. In progress: side project UI, gym 3x. Blocked: waiting on invoice approval. Next: schedule dentist, finish reading notes, prep presentation. Mood this week: mostly steady, one rough Wednesday afternoon. Overall a productive seven days.

entries.title: Creativity block and how I broke it
entries.body: Sat staring at a blank canvas for an hour. Eventually gave up trying to make something good and just made something. Painted a bad landscape. It freed something up. The second piece came easier, then a third. Permission to be bad is the most underrated creative tool I know.

entries.title: Belief audit
entries.body: Wrote down five beliefs I hold about myself that I've never questioned. Most of them came from someone else's voice, probably age 8 to 12. Three of the five don't hold up to evidence. That's uncomfortable. Not sure what to do with this yet but naming them feels like a start.

entries.title: Mood check — low energy week
entries.body: Energy has been flat all week. Not sad exactly, more like greyscale. Appetite okay, sleep okay. Probably the shift to shorter days. Noticed I've been avoiding social messages and reaching for my phone compulsively. Going to cut screen time after 9pm and see if that helps over the next week.

entries.title: Q4 goal check-in
entries.body: Set three goals for Q4: ship the side project, read six books, run a 10K. Side project is 60% done. Books: two finished, one in progress. Running: not started yet. Need to actually block time on the calendar or the 10K will stay a wish. December deadline feels real now.

entries.title: Sunday morning spiritual check
entries.body: Sat in silence for twenty minutes before the rest of the house woke up. No agenda, no timer. Just listened. There's a quality of stillness on Sunday mornings that feels sacred without needing to be religious. Something in me settles. I want to protect this time fiercely going forward.

entries.title: Habit stack update
entries.body: Habit stack is holding: coffee → journal → ten minutes of reading. Streak is 18 days. Added a new one: after lunch, five minutes outside. It's tiny but it breaks the afternoon slump. The goal isn't the streak, it's the automaticity. Starting to feel wrong when I skip rather than optional.

entries.title: Productivity deep work session
entries.body: Four hours of deep work this afternoon, best block I've had in weeks. Phone in another room, notifications off, headphones on with brown noise. Finished the architecture draft and half the data model. The secret is just getting the friction down enough that starting doesn't require willpower.

entries.title: Inner child — the kid who hid
entries.body: Trying something new: writing a letter to my ten-year-old self. Found myself writing about how it was okay to need attention, that wanting to be seen wasn't shameful. Cried a little, which surprised me. There's more here than I realised. Going to sit with this and maybe write back from his perspective.

entries.title: Gratitude — people edition
entries.body: Three people I'm grateful for this week: my friend who checked in without prompting, the colleague who flagged my mistake privately, the stranger who helped me with directions without making it weird. Gratitude for people feels different from gratitude for things. Warmer. More motivating.

entries.title: Brain dump — November noise
entries.body: Thinking about: the conversation I handled badly on Tuesday, whether I'm spending enough time on things that matter, the strange flatness I feel between tasks. Also: need to renew insurance, call about the prescription, fix the drawer handle. Both levels of thought feel equally loud right now.

entries.title: Sleep was rough
entries.body: Woke at 3am, couldn't get back to sleep until 5. Mind was running through tomorrow's presentation on a loop. Tried breathing exercises — helped slightly. Eventually read for twenty minutes and that worked. Going to bed earlier tonight. Consistent sleep time matters more than duration, apparently. Testing that theory.

entries.title: Currently reading: The Body Keeps the Score
entries.body: Halfway through The Body Keeps the Score. Dense but essential. The chapter on how trauma rewires threat detection is changing how I think about some of my own reactions. Not comfortable reading. Taking it slowly, with breaks. Some books you don't race through — you metabolise them.

entries.title: Weekly bullets — Nov 26
entries.body: Done: presentation landed well, fixed the sleep issue, 3 gym sessions. In progress: side project backend, reading. Blocked: nothing critical. Notes: felt more like myself this week, the low-energy spell seems to have lifted. Intention for next week: one act of deliberate creativity, even small.

entries.title: Fitness — weight check
entries.body: Down 1.2kg from last month. Slow but real. Been eating closer to home-cooked, which helps more than any specific diet. The gym habit is solid at three times a week. Not chasing a number — chasing how I feel. Feeling stronger is the actual goal and that is definitely happening.

entries.title: Mood tracker — December starts dark
entries.body: First week of December and it's already noticeably darker by 4pm. Mood dips correlate strongly with light exposure for me. Ordered a SAD lamp. Going to use it during morning journaling. Also conscious that the holiday season brings its own emotional load — some joy, some grief, some complicated family stuff.

entries.title: Creativity — short story draft
entries.body: Wrote 600 words of a short story tonight. No idea where it's going but the character feels real already. She's running from something and I don't know what yet. Writing fiction is strange — sometimes you discover the story rather than invent it. Going to let this one breathe before touching it again.

entries.title: Spiritual — year-end reflection
entries.body: Asked myself: what did I actually believe this year? What changed? Found that I believe less in certainty than I used to, which feels like growth not loss. More comfortable with not knowing. The questions feel more alive than the answers. That's different from where I was in January.

entries.title: Productivity — year-end push
entries.body: This is the crunch zone before the holidays. Made a list of everything that must be done before December 20th. Twelve things. Realistic. Blocked them into the calendar in reverse order from the deadline. The clarity of a list this late in the year is surprisingly calming. Execution over planning now.

entries.title: Inner child — the holidays
entries.body: The holidays activate something old in me. Expectation mixed with disappointment, the ghost of Christmas mornings that were never quite right. Writing this out instead of carrying it silently. The adult me can choose differently. I get to decide what the holidays mean now. That's a new and liberating thought.

entries.title: Habits — December audit
entries.body: Habit audit for the month: journaling daily (strong), running (slipped to twice a week), reading (solid), no-phone after 9pm (about 70%). The phone one is hardest in winter when evenings feel empty. Not judging the slip, just noting. January will be a reset, not a punishment.

entries.title: Gratitude — end of year
entries.body: End of year gratitude list: I'm healthy, which I don't take for granted. I made something I'm proud of. I had real conversations that mattered. I changed my mind about something important. I showed up for someone when it counted. That's a full year by the metrics that matter.

entries.title: Dream log — flying again
entries.body: Flying dream, second one this month. This time over a city I didn't recognise but felt completely at home in. No anxiety about height, just motion. Woke up feeling unusually rested and expansive. Flying dreams always leave me with more energy than I went to sleep with. Strange gift.

entries.title: Reading wrap — 2025
entries.body: Read eleven books this year. Favourites: The Body Keeps the Score, Atomic Habits, a novel I won't name but that cracked something open. Wanted to hit twelve — close enough. The reading habit is the one I'm proudest of. Ideas compound. Next year: more fiction, less self-help. Balance.

entries.title: Brain dump — year-end clear
entries.body: Final brain dump of the year. Unfinished things I'm consciously carrying into 2026: the short story, the 10K, the difficult conversation I keep postponing. Things I'm leaving behind: the comparison spiral, the fantasy that I need to be more productive. Entering next year lighter on purpose.

entries.title: New year — three intentions
entries.body: Not resolutions. Intentions. One: finish things. Two: take rest seriously rather than grudgingly. Three: say the honest thing more often. These feel like character work, not performance targets. I'm not tracking streaks. I'm asking, at the end of each month, did I live these? That's the whole system.

entries.title: Mood check — January reset
entries.body: January feels clean. The break helped. Mood is better than mid-December, which says something about rest. I was running on fumes by the 20th. Starting the SAD lamp routine and it may be doing something. Energy by midday is notably better. Taking this as a win and protecting my sleep.

entries.title: Strength training — new programme
entries.body: Started a proper strength programme. Three days: push, pull, legs. First session was humbling — I've lost more ground than I thought. But the first session is always the hardest because it's the one you do without proof it'll work. Logged everything. Starting weight for benchmarks. Building from here.

entries.title: Creativity — back to the short story
entries.body: Picked up the short story from December. Read it cold and found it better than I remembered, which never happens. Added 400 words. The character's secret revealed itself in a line I didn't plan. Now I know where it's going. The gap between sessions can be part of the process.

entries.title: Mindset — scarcity vs abundance
entries.body: Caught myself thinking in scarcity today: if they succeed, there's less for me. Paused and examined it. It doesn't hold up. Someone else publishing a good book doesn't prevent mine. Someone's promotion isn't my demotion. These feel obvious written down but they required active interruption of the default thought.

entries.title: Weekly bullets — Jan 17
entries.body: Done: three gym sessions, started new reading, daily journaling maintained. In progress: short story draft, side project auth flow. Blocked: need feedback on the design before moving forward. Highlight: had a genuinely good conversation that changed my perspective. More of that. Intention: protect the morning routine.

entries.title: Inner child — the people-pleaser
entries.body: Noticed today that I apologised three times before I'd done anything wrong. Automatic, reflexive. The kid who learned that taking up space was risky. Writing this to make it visible. I don't have to earn my place in conversations. I can just be there. Still learning this at 34.

entries.title: Gratitude — body edition
entries.body: Grateful for my body this morning in a way I often forget. It carried me through a tough workout yesterday without complaint. My hands wrote ten pages this week. My lungs handled the cold run. I spend a lot of time criticising this body and not enough thanking it.

entries.title: Spiritual — connecting the dots
entries.body: Thinking about how the same themes keep appearing in my life in different clothes. The need to be understood. The fear of abandonment. The hunger for meaning in ordinary moments. Carl Jung called this the Self trying to become whole. I don't know if that's true but the pattern is real.

entries.title: Productivity — deep work blocks
entries.body: Two 90-minute deep work blocks per day is my sweet spot. Three is possible but leaves me flat by evening. One feels like I'm coasting. 90 minutes, morning and late afternoon, with a hard stop. Everything else is shallow work: email, admin, messages. Protecting the blocks is the whole job.

entries.title: Habit check — one month in
entries.body: One month into the new year. Journaling: 29/31 days. Running: hit the twice-a-week minimum. Reading before bed: strong. Morning no-phone rule: improving but not perfect. The journaling habit is the anchor. Everything else organises around it. If I write, the day has shape. That observation is worth keeping.

entries.title: Dream — labyrinth
entries.body: Labyrinth dream. Every corridor looked like the last one. Wasn't panicking — more methodical, leaving imaginary markers. Woke up thinking about problem-solving modes and how I sometimes stay in the labyrinth of a problem when I should step back and look for the outside. The dream was probably about work.

entries.title: Reading — Middlemarch
entries.body: Fifty pages into Middlemarch. George Eliot's sentences are architecture. The way she holds irony and compassion simultaneously — skewering a character's vanity while still making you love them. Haven't read 19th century fiction in years. Remembering why it matters. The novel is the most complete form of human attention.

entries.title: Brain dump — frustration clear
entries.body: Frustrated by a meeting where I wasn't heard. Not angry — just tired. Dumping it here: they talked over me twice. My idea was recycled ten minutes later by someone louder and celebrated. I know this is common. I still don't like it. Noting it rather than swallowing it.

entries.title: Valentine's — love notes to self
entries.body: Writing something kind about myself today as an experiment. I'm persistent in ways that look like stubbornness from the outside. I listen well when I'm not anxious. I make good coffee and good playlists. I notice when people are struggling. These aren't small things. They're the whole person.

entries.title: Goal update — side project shipped
entries.body: Shipped the side project beta. Small audience, four users, but it works. Every bug I didn't fix before release is a lesson. Learned more from shipping a flawed thing than I would have from another month of polish. Q1 goal achieved. Next: get feedback, iterate, then decide if this continues.

entries.title: Mindset — failure reframe
entries.body: The launch didn't go as planned. Three things broke. Two users left feedback that stung. Sitting with the urge to catastrophise. Reframe: this is data. I made a real thing and got real responses from real people. That's further than most ideas ever get. Tomorrow I fix the first bug.

entries.title: Fitness — first 5K in two years
entries.body: Ran a 5K this morning without stopping. 32 minutes, which is slow, but continuous. This was the Q4 goal I didn't start until January. Better late. My lungs gave out before my legs, which means the cardiovascular system is the ceiling right now. Adding one longer run per week from here.

entries.title: Weekly bullets — Feb 26
entries.body: Done: 5K run, shipped side project, three good deep work sessions. In progress: bug fixes from user feedback, Middlemarch. Blocked: nothing. Mood: better than last week. The frustration from that meeting has settled. Took the win with the project and let it be enough. Intention next week: rest is productive.

entries.title: March — new chapter feeling
entries.body: March has a different light. Something shifted. Not sure if it's the extra minutes of daylight or the fact that the project shipped, but I feel like I'm in a new chapter. Setting one creative intention for the month: finish the short story. Just the first draft. Nothing more.

entries.title: Spiritual — prayer without religion
entries.body: Wondering what prayer means outside of religion. For me it might be this: writing honestly, without performance, to something larger than my own thinking. The journal as a form of unsent letter. Not to a deity necessarily — to the future self, or to the part of me that already knows.

entries.title: Creativity — story finished
entries.body: First draft of the short story is done. 2,800 words. It's messy and the ending is soft but it exists. I made a thing with a beginning, middle, and end. Six months from idea to draft. Feels significant. Letting it sit for two weeks before reading. Then: revise or abandon. Either is fine.

entries.title: Mood — rough patch
entries.body: A few hard days. Not sure why. Nothing catastrophic happened, just a heaviness that arrived without announcement. Doing the basics: sleep, movement, eating real food, talking to someone. Not trying to fix or explain it. Sometimes the weather changes inside and the best you can do is wear a coat.

entries.title: Habits — rebuilding after the dip
entries.body: Coming out of the rough patch. Habit tracking was basically paused for five days. Not beating myself up — that's the trap. Just picking up where I left off. Journaling this morning feels like putting on clothes after being sick. Familiar, grounding, a sign things are returning. Starting small.

entries.title: Inner child — the part that's afraid
entries.body: The rough patch surfaced something: a part of me that genuinely believes things are going to be taken away just when they get good. Happiness as a warning sign. That's exhausting to carry. Naming it doesn't dissolve it but it does stop it from running the show invisibly. Working on it.

entries.title: Reading — Middlemarch finished
entries.body: Finished Middlemarch. The last line destroyed me in the best way. Dorothea's unhistoric acts — the goodness that accumulates without monument. That's the whole argument. Not grand gestures but consistent, ordinary care. A book I'll reread. It changed how I want to think about what a life is for.

entries.title: Fitness — gym PR
entries.body: Hit a new bench press PR today. Added 10kg from my January starting weight. Small number but mine. The programme is working. The consistent-boring-show-up strategy works better than any clever variation. Consistency over intensity, every time. Logged everything. Looking at the numbers is its own kind of satisfaction.

entries.title: Productivity — system review
entries.body: Quarterly system review. What's working: time-blocked deep work, physical inbox for paper, daily journaling as orientation. What isn't: the weekly review keeps slipping to Monday. Calendars are fragmented across too many tools. Action: consolidate to one calendar and move weekly review to Friday at 4pm. Not later.

entries.title: Gratitude — the hard kind
entries.body: Grateful for the rough patch. I know that sounds performative but I mean it. It forced me to look at something I'd been circling for years. The fear of good things. Without those five flat days I wouldn't have written what I wrote on the 16th. Discomfort as signal.

entries.title: Q1 close — what got built
entries.body: Q1 done. What got built: a fitness baseline, a finished story draft, a shipped project, a better relationship with rest, and a name for a pattern I've carried since childhood. Not on any original goal list. Real anyway. Q2 intention: less planning, more presence. Build the thing in front of me.
